creative_brief: Clean, editorial layout. Hero image: a van unloading produce at a hub door, morning light. Farm names in bold inline. One text CTA button below sign-off.
subject: What's actually inside the Brookvale hub
preheader: Three farms, one cold room, and a grocery store that doesn't exist yet, but almost does.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Last Tuesday, a van pulled up to our Brookvale site.

Bundarra Farm unloaded pork belly and fennel salami, packed the same morning they left the farm. Farmer Brown's eggs came next, still warm from the shed. Then Collins Wild Caught dropped off fish pulled from Australian waters days before any supermarket would even see it.

No distribution centre. No 2,400km detour. Straight from farm to hub.

Here's what that actually means for you: you order online, you collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday, and you know exactly which farm your dinner came from. That's the whole model.

We're not open yet. But the farms are ready, the hub is coming, and the waitlist is how you get first access when we do.

No commitment. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

If you want to go further than just being a customer, if you want to own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first, there's a way to do that too. More on that in a few days.

For now, get on the list.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
